title: Build a Day of Eating
description: Apply nutrition knowledge to plan a realistic, evidence-based day of meals.

steps.title: Estimate your energy needs
steps.instruction: Estimate your basal metabolic rate (BMR) and total daily energy expenditure (TDEE). Use the Mifflin-St Jeor equation or an online calculator.
steps.prompt: What is your estimated TDEE? What activity multiplier did you use? (sedentary: 1.2, lightly active: 1.375, moderately active: 1.55)

steps.title: Plan breakfast
steps.instruction: Design a breakfast with at least 20g protein, fiber from whole foods, and at least one color of vegetable or fruit.
steps.prompt: Describe your breakfast. List the approximate macronutrients: protein, carbohydrate, and fat in grams.

steps.title: Plan lunch and dinner
steps.instruction: Follow a similar pattern for lunch and dinner. Aim for variety in protein sources and vegetables across the day.
steps.prompt: Describe lunch and dinner. What is your total estimated protein for the day? Aim for at least 0.8g per kg body weight.

steps.title: Read one food label
steps.instruction: Pick a packaged food you often eat. Read the label carefully.
steps.prompt: What is the serving size? How many servings are in the package? What are the first 3 ingredients? Any surprises?

steps.title: Identify one food system impact
steps.instruction: Look at the foods in your meal plan. Where did they come from? What is the likely environmental impact of one item?
steps.prompt: Pick one food. Estimate its carbon footprint category: low (vegetables, legumes), medium (fish, poultry), high (beef, lamb).

steps.title: Reflect on your eating environment
steps.instruction: What foods are most visible and accessible in your home right now?
steps.prompt: Does your food environment support the meal plan you just designed? What one change to your environment would make eating well easier?
